conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

Let me start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. I'm inquiring about my son Alex, who is 12 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Let me ask you some questions to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

**Does Alex live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's good to know. Now I need to understand your circumstances a bit more.

**Are you responsible for Alex? By this I mean, do you look after him and pay towards the costs of raising him (such as food, clothes, and other living expenses)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I'm responsible for Alex - I look after him and pay for all his needs like food, clothes, school things, and everything else he needs.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent. Now I need to check a few more things:

**Do you usually live in the UK? (This means the UK is your main home where you normally live.)**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK. Manchester is my home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. A few more questions to complete the assessment:

**Is Alex in full-time non-advanced education or approved training? Or does this not apply because he's only 12 and still in school?**

(For context: children under 16 automatically qualify as long as other conditions are met, but I want to confirm Alex's education status.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Oh, he's just 12, so he's still in secondary school - just normal school for his age.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, that's clear. Just a couple more questions:

**Are you or your partner (if you have one) subject to immigration control, or are there any immigration restrictions that say you cannot receive public funds?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, there are no immigration restrictions. We're not subject to immigration control and there's nothing that says I can't receive public funds.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. One final question:

**Is anyone else also claiming Child Benefit for Alex, or is someone else claiming to be the main person responsible for him?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. It's just me - I'm the one looking after him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided, you meet all the eligibility criteria:

✓ Alex is under 16 years old (he's 12)
✓ You're responsible for Alex - you look after him and pay for his needs
✓ Alex lives with you
✓ You usually live in the UK
✓ You have no immigration restrictions preventing you from receiving public funds
✓ No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

You can go ahead and apply for Child Benefit. The application process can be completed online or by post, and you should receive your payment regularly once your claim is approved.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 12 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 12 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 12 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is eligible for Child Benefit. The claimant meets all the required criteria: (1) Alex is under 16 years old (he is 12), which means he qualifies as a child; (2) The claimant is responsible for Alex - they look after him and pay for his living costs including food, clothes, and other needs; (3) Alex lives with the claimant; (4) The claimant usually lives in the UK (Manchester); (5) There are no immigration restrictions preventing the claimant from receiving public funds; (6) No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All eligibility conditions are satisfied.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (age 12), who lives with them in Manchester. The claimant is responsible for Alex, pays for his needs, usually lives in the UK, has no immigration restrictions, and no one else is claiming for Alex. All Child Benefit eligibility criteria are met.
